description: PNW College Baseball Park Factors — physics-driven with home/road validation
methodology: 80% physics (elevation + dimensions + temperature) + 20% home/road splits (regressed). Elevation: +1.8%/1000ft. Temperature: +1%/10F above 60F. Dimensions: +0.15% per foot smaller than 330/400/330 baseline.

teams.notes: 293' down lines, 405' center, 16' wall. MLB-standard lighting. Very dry climate. Named after HOF coach.
HIGHEST elevation park in PNW at 4,108 ft. Significant thin-air effect on ball flight. Cold spring starts.
Very dry climate. One of driest locations in PNW.
High elevation. Cold spring starts. Mountain environment.
First baseball field in Idaho with turf infield. 1,000 purple seat backs (2015).
South side of campus. One of oldest NWAC programs.
Very dry climate. Columbia Basin region.
Dimensions: 330-375-400. 12,500 sq ft hitting facility. One of finest NWAC fields.
Turf infield installed 2022. Named after former coach Elmore Vail.
2013 renovations: synthetic turf, below-ground dugouts. Named after coach Paul Merkel.
On-campus. Rain shadow climate.
Largest capacity venue of any PNW college team. Formerly minor league stadium. In Keizer (adjacent to Salem).
Opened 2008. Shared with Billings Mustangs (Pioneer League). Highest elevation NCAA park in PNW.
First D1 program to install FieldTurf for baseball (2003). First PNW park with lights (1984).
Dimensions: 324-412. Renovated 2019. Known for wind.

teams.stadium: Washington Trust Field at Patterson Baseball Complex
teams.notes: Opened 2007. Named for Washington Trust Bank.
Rain shadow. Semi-arid. Near Idaho border.
$4.9M facility completed 2018. Professional-grade LED lights. At Thunderbird Park on campus.
On main campus. Upgrades planned.
Semi-arid climate. Rain shadow location.
3,500 seats. Historic site — former home of Salem Angels and Salem Dodgers.
5,000-seat capacity. Hosted NAIA World Series 28 times. Center field 385 ft. 19 national titles.
Dimensions: 330-365-385. Named after Al Courter (1994). 100% natural grass.
Off-campus facility shared with community.
Dimensions: 325-380-330. Renamed Funko Field 2019. Electronic scoreboard.
Named after longtime coach (dedicated April 2024). Part of Joe Huston Memorial Sports Complex.
$3M redevelopment. Lighted. Actually in Springfield (adjacent to Eugene).
Coastal marine influence. Very wet. Dense moist air suppresses ball carry.
Dimensions: 320-385. Constructed 2012.
Historic venue inaugurated 1926. Named after Raymond Borleske.
On 101-acre Central Park campus.
Completed 1999. Turf infield installed 2025.
Renovated 2014, AstroTurf surface. Left field borders Union Bay wetlands.
Program returned after 50-year absence in 2025. Shares field with Portland Pilots.
Dimensions: 325-388-325. Turf installed 2014-2015.
Built 1989 in Bush's Pasture Park. Turf additions 2019.
On-campus facility.
Opened 2009, named after Pat Kilkenny. East of Autzen Stadium.

teams.stadium: Chuck Bafaro Stadium at Bond Field
teams.notes: Dimensions: 330-390. Pitcher's park. Built 2008 in Lincoln Park.

teams.stadium: Chuck Bafaro Stadium at Bond Field
teams.notes: Dimensions: 330-390. Pitcher's park. Built 2008 in Lincoln Park.
Located in Bellevue, ~10 miles from campus. Small 300-seat venue.
All-natural playing surface. Named after groundskeeper Sam Minnitti. Premier NWAC facility. First season 1979.
WETTEST location in PNW college baseball. County-managed park adjacent to campus.
Oldest continuous ballpark in the nation (opened 1907). Renovated 2015.
Oldest continuous ballpark in the nation (opened 1907). Renovated 2015.
4-acre complex completed 2023. Modern turf with drainage system.
Located in Surrey, not at New Westminster campus.

teams.stadium: Lakewood Baseball Field at Fort Steilacoom Park
teams.notes: First-ever home field for program (first game April 2024). At Fort Steilacoom campus, not Puyallup.
Off-campus facility.
Recent $1.5M renovation gift for facility improvements.

teams.stadium: Curtis and Margaret Morse Athletic Fields
teams.notes: Turf infield since 2016. Used since 1989.
On-campus. 69 baseball players drafted historically.
Brand new turf field (first game Feb 14, 2026). Dimensions: 320-405-312.
Lights added 2000. Dedicated 1991. Crescent-shaped grandstand.
Drainage system installed 2018. New dugouts added 2005.
Marine influence from Puget Sound.
New artificial turf. Allows play rain or shine. Formerly Legion Memorial Field.
WETTEST team location in PNW. Coastal Pacific influence. Also uses Ken Waite Field in Aberdeen.
Gem of campus. AstroTurf infield (Jan 2026). Grandstand from original Seattle Pilots. Hosted 23 of 41 NWAACC championships.
